questions.q: In Switzerland, it is illegal to own just one of which popular household pet?
Which country has held the title of the world's happiest nation for seven consecutive years?
Finland has more of which item than it does cars?
Which celestial body is smaller in diameter than the continent of Australia?
How many time zones does Russia span across its territory?
What was penicillin humorously referred to as before becoming known as a miracle drug?
Approximately how many calories does the human brain burn per day?
Which country is famous for creating unique flavored Kit Kats, including sushi-inspired varieties?
The letter Q appears in the names of how many U.S. states?
Which marine mammal holds hands with other members of its species while sleeping?
What percentage of orange-colored domestic cats are male?
Dogs cool themselves down primarily by sweating through which part of their body?
In which European city is the Eiffel Tower located?
According to common folklore, approximately how many spiders might a person swallow while sleeping per year?
What surprising substance does a platypus secrete through its skin?
Which U.S. state is known as the smallest state by area?
When heated, metals undergo expansion due to a physical phenomenon called what?
Under ultraviolet light, which common fruit glows with a blue luminescence?
Which crustacean has its heart located in its head rather than in its chest?
What is the only metal that is liquid at room temperature?
Which country has more kangaroos than people?
What color is the blood of an octopus?
How many hearts does a squid have?
Which animal can sleep while only half of its brain rests?
What is the only bird that can fly backwards?
Which country has a flag with no straight lines?
How many bones are in an adult human body?
Which planet rotates backwards compared to most other planets?
What percentage of the Earth's oxygen is produced by the ocean?
Which mammal lays eggs instead of giving birth?
How many eyes does a bee have?
What is the only continent with no active volcanoes?
Which animal has fingerprints nearly identical to human fingerprints?
How many muscles does a caterpillar have?
How many times does your heart beat in a single day?
Which animal uniquely sweats milk instead of through traditional sweat glands?
If stretched out end to end, how far would all the hair on an average human head reach?
In which country would you find more saunas than cars?
How frequently does Japan have vending machines in relation to its population?
Which ocean feature is located near Australia?
What is the scientific name for the fear of other people's judgment?
Which film script was famously sold for just one dollar?
What unusual flavor did McDonald's once test in broccoli products?
Jennifer Lopez's green dress at which awards ceremony inspired the creation of Google Images?
Before becoming the famous space ranger, what was Buzz Lightyear's original name in early Toy Story drafts?
Which of these countries has a national anthem with no words?
Which ancient sea creature has blue blood due to copper in its veins?
In which body part is a shrimp's heart located?
Which animal can change its gender multiple times during its lifetime?
Which of these creatures uses echolocation to navigate in the dark?
Which is the largest butterfly species in the world?
What is the approximate length in letters of the longest word in the English language, a chemical term for a type of protein?
Which iconic monument was originally commissioned and designed to be constructed in Barcelona before its location was changed to Paris?
Which country is so geographically small that it fits entirely within the city of Rome and functions as an independent nation-state?
Do human fingernails or toenails grow at a faster rate?
In what year did Japan first release sushi-flavored Kit Kats as part of a regional candy flavor experiment?
Which African country stretches across all four hemispheres of the Earth, making it the only nation to do so?
What is the only organ in the human body that cannot be legally donated to the Pope according to Vatican law?
Which artist created over 55 self-portraits during the 20th century, predating modern selfie culture by decades?
Which chicken survived for 18 months after having its head severed in 1945?
Which body part grows faster than any other continuously-replenishing tissue in the human body?
What unusual substance does a platypus secrete from its skin that resembles milk?
Which U.S. state contains more pyramidal structures than Egypt?
Which animator holds the record for the most Academy Award wins by any individual?
What is the name of the phobia describing the fear of long words themselves?
How long would it theoretically take to fall through Earth to its core without air resistance?
Which mammal is the only one known to be incapable of voluntarily moving its ears?
In what year did the Eiffel Tower, originally intended for Barcelona, instead debut at the Paris Exposition Universelle?
Frida Kahlo created at least 55 self-portraits during her lifetime. What iconic physical feature appears prominently in many of her works?
The # symbol used in modern internet hashtags derives its formal name from a term originating in typography. What is this technical name?
Which fruit contains the highest protein content per 100 grams among all commonly consumed fruits?
Horatio Magellan Crunch is the full name of which beloved breakfast cereal mascot?
When exposed to ultraviolet light, bananas emit a fluorescence of what color?
Approximately how many species of insects are estimated to exist on Earth?
The continent of Africa spans all four hemispheres. Which of the following best describes its hemispheric reach?
The Moon's diameter is smaller than the width of which Australian landform?
How many miles of blood vessels does an adult human's circulatory system typically span when measured end-to-end?
What unexpected physiological effect can result from wearing a tight necktie for extended periods?
choices: Reduced blood flow by up to 7.5%

questions.q: Before ketchup became a staple condiment in the 20th century, it was marketed as what type of product?
Approximately what percentage of the global human population is naturally left-handed?
National Geographic, the renowned exploration and documentation organization, was founded in what year?
Which organelle within human cells contains its own DNA separate from the nucleus, leading scientists to theorize it was once an independent organism?
In what year did the Chicxulub asteroid impact occur, marking the extinction event that wiped out non-avian dinosaurs?
Which element has the highest melting point of all known elements, making it extraordinarily difficult to work with in laboratories?
In what language was the most widely-spoken linguistic system historically used in the Ottoman Empire's administrative courts?
Which deep-sea fish species produces light through bioluminescence and possesses a distinctive modified dorsal fin ray that functions as a fishing rod?
In quantum mechanics, what principle states that the product of the uncertainties in measuring a particle's position and momentum cannot fall below a specific limit?
Which artistic movement, originating in early 20th-century Europe, deliberately rejected rational logic and embraced the irrational and absurd as a response to World War I?
What is the name of the thick, protein-rich layer of the Earth's interior that lies between the crust and the outer core?
Which monarch of England established the Church of England primarily as a means to dissolve his marriage to Catherine of Aragon?
In 1960, McDonald's tested a controversial flavor variant of which vegetable that was never widely released?
The small pocket found on most blue jeans was originally designed to hold what object in the late 1800s?
Which mythical horned creature is the official national animal of Scotland?
Snakes detect odors primarily through which body part?
Which country contains more pyramidal structures than Egypt?
Which part of the adult human body cannot repair itself once damaged?
In the 1800s, which dangerous element was applied topically by beauty enthusiasts to achieve pale skin?
The creation of Google Images was directly inspired by search queries about which celebrity's fashion moment at the 2000 Grammy Awards?
The blue whale possesses a tongue that can weigh as much as how many elephants?
What unusual compound gives comets their distinctive odor when they approach the sun?
The platypus is remarkable partly because it is one of the few mammals that produces milk through which unusual method?
How many self-portraits did artist Frida Kahlo create during her lifetime?
Which creature possesses a tongue that can weigh as much as an elephant?
Which continental region is home to the Great Barrier Reef?
How many countries are located on the African continent?
Which African nation contains more pyramids than Egypt?
By what percentage can a tight necktie reduce blood flow to the brain?
What surprising ability do sea otters display while sleeping in the ocean?
Which of the following is the largest continent by land area?
Bottlenose dolphins are unique among marine mammals for exhibiting which form of communication?
Which reptile uses its forked tongue primarily as a sensory organ to detect chemical particles in the air and ground?
The Eiffel Tower was originally commissioned for construction in which European city before being built in Paris?
What is the diameter of the Moon in relation to the width of Australia?
Which planet in our solar system experiences surface temperatures hot enough to melt lead, despite being farther from the Sun than Mercury?
In which country is it legally required to own at least two guinea pigs if you own any at all?
Sea otters are famous for holding hands while sleeping to prevent themselves from drifting apart. What is another critical reason they engage in this behavior?
choices: To protect their young from predators

questions.q: Which of the following citrus fruits would sink in water while its counterpart floats?
Fingernails grow approximately how much faster than toenails over the course of a year?
